reforms.description: Since 2004, all enterprises in Turkey can deduct 40% of eligible R&D investment from their taxable income, and firms established in designated sites near universities are exempt from corporate taxes for their software and R&D sales. The government plans to assess the efficiency of these tax incentives for research-and-development and technoparks, aiming to improve their effectiveness in fostering innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives are designed to stimulate R&D investment, which is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax deduction and exemptions have been in effect since 2004.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the tax landscape for R&D investments, encouraging more firms to engage in research activities.
reforms.source_quote: Since 2004, all enterprises in Turkey can also deduct 40% of eligible R&D investment from their taxable income.

reforms.description: The Turkish government reduced the value-added tax (VAT) rate on textile products from 18% to 8% to support the textile sector facing competition from low-cost countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering VAT is expected to enhance competitiveness and support the textile sector's growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The VAT reduction has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the VAT reduction is a targeted measure rather than a broad reform.
reforms.source_quote: The government reduced the value-added tax (VAT) rate on textile products from the standard 18% to a preferential 8%, starting from March 2006.

reforms.description: An 'Agricultural Strategy' was adopted and transformed into an 'Agricultural Law' in 2006, emphasizing direct income support as the primary instrument while allowing for deficiency payments to maintain national production capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance agricultural productivity and sustainability through direct support mechanisms.
reforms.status_evidence: The Agricultural Law has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The transformation of the strategy into law represents a significant policy shift in agricultural support.
reforms.source_quote: Starting from the understanding that accelerating diversification into new products, improving quality and enhancing productivity require a more pro-active approach, an 'Agricultural Strategy' was adopted by the government and transformed into an 'Agricultural Law' in 2006.

reforms.description: Technology transfer services are proposed to enhance the technological upgrading of Turkish agriculture by disseminating information on high value-added products and their production technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating technology transfer is expected to improve agricultural productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal for technology transfer services has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important for agricultural innovation, the proposal is not yet implemented.
reforms.source_quote: Technology transfer services which have to date played a relatively limited role in the technological upgrading of Turkish farming should help to diffuse information on higher value-added and market-demanded products.
